title: At least 7 burnt alive, several injured after blast at illegal fireworks godown in Jaipur
content: A devastating blast and subsequent fire at an alleged illegal fireworks godown in Jaipur's Khoh Nagoriyan area claimed at least seven lives on Tuesday morning. Several others sustained injuries in the incident. Authorities are investigating the cause, with safety lapses and illegal storage being considered.

title: Who is Totoa Auvaa? 125kg rugby player tried to hit Stokes' teammate in nightclub row
content: The England and Wales Cricket Board (ECB) confirmed on Monday that the pair were present at a nightclub in the early hours after the match and that an incident took place.

title: India deploys 12 nuclear warheads for first time; where do China and Russia stand?
content: India has deployed 12 nuclear warheads for the first time, a significant shift in its nuclear posture, according to SIPRI. With a total of 190 warheads, this move signals a move towards a more operationally ready sea-based deterrent. This development occurs as global nuclear arsenals expand, with China rapidly increasing its stockpile.

title: TCS chairman N Chandrasekaran says company has no layoff plans
content: TCS Chairman N Chandrasekaran confirmed no layoffs and a continued hiring strategy, though massive campus hiring may cease. He emphasized AI as a significant opportunity, not a threat, with AI revenues nearing $2.5 billion annually and projected to be 100% of revenue by 2028-2030.  AI is seen as intelligence infrastructure, creating new opportunities and talent needs.

title: IND A vs SL A: Prabhsimran's costly blunder sparks controversy; Gaikwad furious
content: India A players were left fuming after a controversial run-out decision in their Tri-Nation A Series clash against Sri Lanka A. Wicketkeeper Prabhsimran Singh dislodged the bails before the ball hit the stumps, leading to Chamika Karunaratne being given not out despite appearing short of his crease.

title: Layoffs announced at Sam Altman's $2.5 billion startup amid revenue struggles
content: Sam Altman's eyeball-scanning startup, Tools for Humanity, is reportedly cutting jobs as it navigates its next strategic phase. Despite a significant valuation and user sign-ups, the company faces challenges in demonstrating revenue generation and regulatory approval for its "Orb" technology. This news emerges as OpenAI, also led by Altman, files for its IPO.

title: Russian crude here to stay? Why India-US energy ties may be more about LPG, LNG than oil
content: Geopolitical developments in the last few months have dictated India’s energy supplies. Be it crude oil, LPG or LNG, India is largely dependent on the world to meet its needs, and the US-Iran war has disrupted supplies via the Strait of Hormuz.

title: From Parliament to premieres: Kangana Ranaut's saree love is changing fashion conversations
content: Kangana Ranaut is revitalizing the saree's relevance by consistently wearing Indian weaves for everyday occasions, from Parliament to film promotions. Her practical styling and embrace of handloom fabrics demonstrate that traditional attire can be both modern and professional, challenging the notion that sarees are solely for special events.

title: Mark Zuckerberg says US needs thousands of trade workers to power AI boom
content: Mark Zuckerberg announced Meta's America's Workforce Academy (AWA), a new initiative offering free training and job pathways in skilled trades. This program aims to address the projected need for hundreds of thousands of trade workers to build the infrastructure for the United States' AI ambitions. Meta is partnering with various organizations to deliver this crucial training.

title: Arjun Rampal recalls darkest phase amid separation and mother’s cancer battle
content: Arjun Rampal candidly shared the profound emotional turmoil following his divorce and the loss of his mother to cancer, describing it as a dark, lonely period. He found solace and strength in his relationship with Gabriella Demetriades, with whom he weathered the storm and built a new life, now parents to two sons.

title: Hero MotoCorp buyer gets Rs 88,000 refund over recurring engine issue
content: A Bengaluru consumer court ordered Hero MotoCorp and its dealers to refund Rs 88,000 for a motorcycle with a recurring engine defect. Despite over 10 service visits and numerous part replacements, the bike's engine repeatedly shut off while riding. The court found a manufacturing defect, citing the company's own admission of the issue during test rides.

title: TN CM Vijay launches Special Force, says drones will be used to prevent crimes against women
content: The chief minister linked crimes against women with the growing menace of drug trafficking. He said the govt would take strong measures to eradicate the drug network and improve law and order.

title: Tragic end for Aggarwal family: After losing 8 relatives in Delhi fire, 75-year-old dies at hospital
content: A devastating Delhi hotel fire tragically claimed the lives of eight members of the Aggarwal family, including a son, daughter-in-law, and grandchildren. The sole survivor, 75-year-old Radhe Shyam Aggarwal, has since passed away. The blaze, which killed 22 in total, exposed severe safety lapses at the Flourish Inn, including illegal construction and a lack of fire systems.

title: NEET paper leak accused seeks interim bail to appear for re-exam
content: A Delhi court is considering an interim bail plea for NEET-UG paper leak accused Yash Yadav, who seeks to attend the re-examination. The court has allowed him study materials and will hear the CBI's response on June 12. The investigation has revealed a network distributing leaked papers for profit across multiple states.

title: Iran deal 'very close'? 37 times Trump's predictions fell flat
content: President Trump repeatedly declared a deal with Iran imminent for months, even after a ceasefire was announced. Despite his optimistic pronouncements, tensions persist as Iran launched retaliatory attacks following Israeli strikes, straining a fragile ceasefire and raising questions about US influence.

title: Bills eye former saints all-pro in potential offensive upgrade
content: The Buffalo Bills have been linked to a potential trade for New Orleans Saints running back Alvin Kamara after FOX Sports insider Greg Auman named Buffalo as a possible landing spot. Kamara, now 30, is coming off a down season and carries an $11.5 million salary for 2026. While the veteran still offers value as a pass-catching back, many around the league question whether adding him would truly address Buffalo’s need for more explosive offensive playmakers.

title: Love quote of the day: Taylor Swift on the gesture that changed everything
content: Taylor Swift's new song for 'Toy Story 5' and her rumored wedding to Travis Kelce are making headlines. Swift described Kelce's bold approach to dating her as a dream come true, reminiscent of an '80s movie. Their relationship timeline highlights key moments, from his initial admiration to their engagement.

title: Watch: Bat blocked by Dickwella's foot? Priyansh Arya's freak run-out triggers debate
content: A strange mix-up saw Priyansh Arya run out in the tri-series opener against Sri Lanka A.  His bat collided with the wicketkeeper's foot while trying to make his ground.  Ruturaj Gaikwad scored a century to rescue India A after an early collapse.  Late contributions helped India A post a competitive total.

title: Fear of recurrence: How immune memory helps cancer survivors face their worst nightmare
content: As we observe National Cancer Survivors Month, we delve into an exciting new era in the battle against cancer recurrence: the remarkable memory of our immune systems. Research indicates that our bodies can recognize and target cancer cells long after traditional treatment has concluded.

title: Karnataka horror: Woman drugged, 'gang-raped' and filmed; 10 arrested
content: Police arrested ten individuals in Davangere, Karnataka, following a disturbing incident. A 41-year-old woman was allegedly drugged and gang-raped in a village. The crime came to light after a video surfaced online. The survivor filed a complaint, leading to the swift arrests. Investigations are ongoing to gather further evidence and trace the video's circulation.

title: Salman Khan controls his tears as he attends close friend's funeral with family
content: Salman Khan and his family attended the funeral of their longtime friend Kumud Rane, with the actor appearing visibly emotional. This loss follows the recent passing of Sushil Kumar, a close friend of over four decades whom Salman considered a brother. Sushil was remembered for his unwavering positivity and kindness.

title: Simone Biles & Jonathan Owens' 2026 net worth and luxury lifestyle
content: Simone Biles and Jonathan Owens have an estimated combined net worth of $27 million in 2026. Simone accounts for about $25 million through endorsements, appearances, tours, and investments, while Jonathan’s $2 million comes mainly from NFL contracts. The couple also owns valuable real estate and enjoys a luxury lifestyle, helping secure their financial future beyond sports.

title: Atal Asra Yojana: Goa's housing scheme for tribal communities explained
content: Goa's Atal Asra Yojana offers up to Rs 3 lakh for Scheduled Tribe families to build, reconstruct, or repair homes. Eligibility requires belonging to a Goan ST community with an annual family income not exceeding Rs 3 lakh. Applications are offline at the Tribal Welfare office in Panaji, requiring specific documents for assistance.

title: NEET UG 2026 re-test: Pradhan reviews NTA readiness, tightens security and coordination
content: NEET UG 2026: Union Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan reviewed NEET-UG 2026 re-test preparations at NTA headquarters in Delhi ahead of June 21. As per ANI, he stressed strict confidentiality across all stages, from question paper setting to distribution. He highlighted coordination with departments, states and central agencies, including a Cabinet Secretary-led meeting and a planned review by the Union Home Secretary. He also noted new administrative appointments and cyber security measures involving IB and CBI support for examination integrity.

title: Poaching fears? Congress relocates Madhya Pradesh MLAs to Karnataka, ahead of RS polls
content: Amidst Rajya Sabha polls in Madhya Pradesh, Congress MLAs are being moved to Karnataka, with party leaders alleging BJP poaching attempts. Congress claims BJP offered bribes to its legislators. The move aims to prevent cross-voting and secure their candidate's victory, recalling the 2020 government collapse.

title: Setback for Trump as US judge strikes down $100,000 fee: How it impacts H-1B visa aspirants
content: A US federal judge has blocked President Trump's controversial $100,000 fee for new H-1B visas. The judge ruled the administration overstepped its authority by imposing the charge, calling it an unauthorized tax. This decision offers relief to many aspiring foreign workers, particularly from India, and companies relying on skilled talent.

title: Jude Bellingham's attitude questioned over England bench role at World Cup
content: England midfielder Jude Bellingham could face an unexpected challenge at the FIFA World Cup 2026, with former Three Lions defender Conor Coady questioning how the Real Madrid star would react if he is left out of Thomas Tuchel’s starting lineup.England begin their World Cup campaign against Croatia on June 17. Ghana and Panama are the other teams in Group L.

title: DU professor's murder: Recces, WhatsApp calls and forged IDs point to a plot mapped in advance
content: A Delhi University professor's murder on June 3 appears to have been premeditated by her tenants, who were reportedly angered by her refusal to sell her ancestral home. The couple allegedly conducted reconnaissance and planned the murder after the professor agreed to meet them on June 3.

title: DK Shivakumar takes bites from apples, throws them into crowd; video goes viral
content: Karnataka chief minister DK Shivakumar's public gesture of biting apples from a ceremonial garland and tossing them to supporters has sparked a viral debate. While some hail it as an affectionate interaction, others criticize the act of distributing partially eaten food. The incident, captured in Kanakapura, has drawn mixed reactions online, prompting discussions about its appropriateness.

title: TDS on property purchase above Rs 50 lakh explained: Rules, conditions and filing process
content: Property buyers purchasing assets valued at Rs 50 lakh or more must deduct 1% TDS before completing the transaction. This rule, under Section 194-IA, applies to non-agricultural land and aims to boost tax compliance in real estate. Buyers must deposit the tax and issue a certificate to the seller, ensuring accurate reporting of high-value deals.

title: Siraj ruled out of Ireland and England T20Is; Krishna named replacement
content: India pacer Mohammed Siraj has been withdrawn from the T20I squads for the Ireland and England tours as part of a workload management programme. The BCCI said the move was precautionary to ensure adequate recovery before a busy season. Prasidh Krishna has been named as his replacement in the Shreyas Iyer-led squad, which also includes teenage sensation Vaibhav Sooryavanshi.

title: Zojila tunnel breakthrough: Road Pakistan wanted to choke during Kargil gets all-weather shield
content: A major milestone has been achieved in the Zojila Tunnel project, connecting its two ends to create a 13-km passage. This vital infrastructure will ensure all-weather connectivity between Kashmir and Ladakh, eliminating annual disruptions and significantly reducing travel time. Expected to be operational by 2028, the tunnel is crucial for military logistics and regional development.

title: Strait of Hormuz closure: Why high oil prices may be a temporary shock only - explained
content: “Oil prices will be lower if Hormuz reopens earlier. Uncertainty remains high regarding the timing of Hormuz reopening, and oil prices will remain volatile as a result,” Fitch says.

title: Court orders AirAsia to pay farmer Rs 90,750 after delayed flight damaged rare jackfruit plant
content: A farmer's hybrid jackfruit sapling was ruined after AirAsia's flight delay caused him to miss his connection. The consumer commission ordered AirAsia to pay Rs 90,750 for the damaged sapling, refunding ticket fare, reimbursing expenses, and compensating for mental agony and deficiency in service. The airline failed to appear, leading to an ex-parte ruling.

title: Jagapathi Babu thanks 'Peddi' critics for negative reviews of the film
content: Jagapathi Babu lauded Ram Charan's pivotal role in 'Peddi', calling him 'Superman' for shouldering the film. He also humorously thanked critics for negative reviews, stating they inadvertently boosted the movie's earnings. The film's success was celebrated, with Babu highlighting the team's resilience in overcoming challenges. Read on to know more in detail.

title: Deve Gowda had never sought Rajya Sabha seat, says Union Minister H.D. Kumaraswamy
content: Janata Dal (Secular) leader and Union Minister for Heavy Industries and Steel H.D. Kumaraswamy has clarified that former Prime Minister H.D. Deve Gowda never asked their alliance partner, the Bharatiya Janata Party, for ticket to contest the Rajya Sabha elections

title: Failure to read political trends and course correct in real time resulted in LDF’s defeat, feels CPI(M)
content: Failure to call out Vellappally Natesan’s anti-Muslim invective, the covert Congress-BJP alliance, and the delay in expelling Padmakumar in the Sabarimala case, caste identity politics pivoting towards a majoritarian Hindutva agenda, and CPI(M)’s organisational weaknesses compounded the scale of defeat

title: Farmers stage protest in Kalaburagi seeking adequate supply of DAP
content: They say shortage has been artificially created to facilitate black market sales ahead of kharif sowing season

title: YSRCP seeks relief for mango growers hit by low prices
content: Nagi Reddy says falling prices, lower yields and weak demand from pulp industries have left Totapuri growers struggling to recover cultivation costs

title: Four burnt alive after fire at godown in Jaipur's Khoh Nagoriyan
content: “Four deaths have been confirmed so far, and four persons are injured. Prima facie, it appears to be a storage facility," says Police Commissioner

title: India urges global community to hold Pakistan accountable after PoK violence
content: “We continue to see in this context, a pattern of fake news and videos emanating from Pakistan. It is a desperate attempt by Pakistan to cover-up its own failings and deflect attention away from its human rights abuses,” MEA spokesperson Randhir Jaiswal said

title: Kerala wild elephant attack: forest department takes responsibility for  victim’s children
content: The State government will provide an initial financial assistance of ₹5 lakh to the family; a Forest department official will be appointed as a dedicated mentor to personally look after the needs of the children

title: Naidu to attend NDA, NITI Aayog meetings in New Delhi
content: Chief Minister to take part in alliance partners’ meeting on June 10 and NITI Aayog Governing Council meeting the next day; Andhra Pradesh development priorities likely to figure in discussions

title: BMTC to appoint consultant for smart ticketing project
content: BMTC officials say the consultant will be tasked with monitoring the performance of the technology solution provider engaged for the project and ensuring compliance with contractual, technical, cybersecurity and operational requirements

title: ED provisionally attaches ₹100 crore assets in Jaypee Infratech-Jaiprakash Associates case
content: About ₹32,825 crore was collected by JIL and JAL from homebuyers, of which substantial amounts had been diverted and siphoned off to related entities, says ED

title: Bengaluru rowdy-sheeter found murdered in Hassan
content: The deceased, Manjesh Kumar, faced criminal charges in multiple cases registered under the Arms Act and the SC/ST Prevention of Atrocities Act at Thalaghattapura Police Station in Bengaluru

title: Vijay to attend NITI Aayog Governing Council meeting in Delhi
content: Sources said Mr. Vijay is scheduled to leave Chennai for New Delhi on a special flight on Wednesday. In New Delhi, he is expected to meet some political leaders.

title: Here are the big stories from Karnataka today
content: Welcome to the Karnataka Today newsletter, your guide from The Hindu on the major news stories to follow today. Curated and written by Nalme Nachiyar.

title: Leader of the Opposition in Kerala Pinarayi Vijayan refutes Major Ravi's claim that he attended actor Innocent's funeral with a huge convoy
content: Office of the Leader of the Opposition says that the statement is factually incorrect and necessary legal action will be initiated against those responsible for disseminating such lies and false content through social media platforms

title: Chennai power cuts | Some people instigating residents to protest even after restoration of supply: Minister Nirmalkumar
content: He added that additional teams have been deployed to restore power in areas including Perambur and Villivakkam

title: In Bengaluru, a garbage crisis deepens
content: The is a four-part series on Bengaluru’s mounting solid waste management woes, compounded by an ineffective garbage collection system.

title: Why is there a rebellion in the Trinamool Congress? | Explained
content: What event triggered the rebellion within the Trinamool Congress legislature party? Why were MLAs Ritabrata Banerjee and Sandipan Saha expelled from the party? What challenges do Mamata Banerjee and the party face?

title: Rebel TMC MPs siding with BJP, says Kalyan Banerjee
content: ‘Today their leader is Narendra Modi; they have become BJP,’ says Kalyan Banerjee, Chief Whip of AITC in the Lok Sabha, on the rebel Trinamool Congress MPs

title: Kumaraswamy announces ₹25 lakh and a job for kin of Visakhapatnam steel plant blast victims
content: The Steel Minister visits RINL and the hospital, orders an independent inquiry, and announces jobs and aid for the families of the dead and the injured

title: EV dream meets reality: Karnataka’s electric vehicle users face charging, cost and infrastructure hurdles
content: While Bengaluru leads Karnataka’s transition towards electric mobility, vehicle owners say inadequate charging infrastructure, long charging times, and high purchase cost continue to pose challenges for wider adoption. Darshan Devaiah and Mini Tejaswi take an overview of the EV scenario on World Environment Day

title: Pawan Kalyan questions VSP safety after blast, flags similar incident the night before
content: Deputy CM stresses need for upgradation of VSP and also expresses displeasure over delay in allotment of captive mines; Decides to hold a meet with workers union soon

title: No incriminating material found in data retrieved from TNPDCL’s stolen hard disks: Police
content: While 18 hard disks were reported stolen, the police found 34 hard disks in the possession of the suspect in Bengaluru. The case has been transferred to CB-CID from the Greater Chennai Police

title: How civic agency-contractor tussle cripples Bengaluru’s waste collection
content: Drivers and waste collection workers have become highly irregular in their attendance due to payment-related issues

title: ‘Not just about giving money’: Lokesh pledges long-term support for steel blast families
content: The IT Minister says the families will get the first ₹25 lakh at once and talks in Amaravati later, and rejects Opposition charges linking the accident to VSP’s privatisation

title: Chola and Hoysala idols at risk during Egmore Museum renovation
content: Ancient sculptures and temple pillars seized and repatriated by the Tamil Nadu Idol Wing lie lye amid construction equipment during ongoing renovation work at Chennai’s Egmore Government Museum, prompting concerns

title: Seven years after floods, dozens of tribal families still live under tarpaulin roofs
content: Families continue to live in makeshift shelters despite receiving land titles months ago. While a new bridge over the Chaliyar River is nearing completion, allowing pedestrian access, the lack of permanent housing continues to leave residents exposed.

title: West Bengal CID reaches Mamata Banerjee’s residence in ‘forged signatures’ probe
content: After a standoff with party supporters, a team of half a dozen CID officials entered the residence of the former CM

title: Rajya Sabha polls: Amid fears of cross-voting, Madhya Pradesh Congress MLAs leave for Bengaluru
content: Various Congress MLAs have alleged in the media that they are ‘being approached on behalf of the BJP with lucrative offers’

title: Movement restricted in Jaipur's Jagatpura area after demolition of 5 religious structures
content: The demolition exercise, carried out on June 8 amid tight security arrangements, was completed peacefully, and authorities were engaged in removing the debris from the site to facilitate the widening of the road running parallel to a railway line

title: Study unveils new fish species in Western Ghats, solves evolutionary riddle
content: Eechathalakenda incognita, the newly discovered species, had been confused with Eechathalakenda ophicephala for the last 70 years

title: Kerala govt seeks Centre’s permission to use forest land for Wayanad MCH expansion
content: This is in return for a similar parcel of land. Health Minister open State-level launch of ‘Kayakalpam’ initiative to gather public opinion on deficiencies in health system and patient care services

title: Rs 1.23 Lakh Crore In 78 Days: Sources On Support To Oil Firms After Iran War
content: After the first 78 days, the Ministry of Finance determined that extending further financial support to a single sector would not be appropriate, the sources said.

title: No Trump-Putin Call Planned, No Timeline On US Envoys' Visit: Russia
content: American negotiators Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner continue to maintain contacts with both Russia and Ukraine, Kremlin spokesman Dmitry Peskov said.

title: India's Nuclear Arsenal Swells To 190, Surpasses Pakistan, Israel, N Korea
content: For the first time, India has kept 12 nuclear warheads deployed during peacetime, breaking with its longstanding practice of storing warheads separately from delivery systems such as ballistic missiles, according to SIPRI.

title: 8 Relatives Died In Delhi Fire, Now Patriarch Dies, Family Wiped Out
content: Radhe Shyam Aggarwal had been inquiring about the absence of his family members at the hospital, left completely unaware of what had happened just a few hundred metres away from the hospital.

title: US Sanctions Alibaba, BYD, Baidu In Crackdown On Chinese Companies
content: The list, updated and published Monday by the Pentagon, now sanctions well-known, non-state-owned Chinese companies that are not traditionally considered to be in the defence or security sector.

title: 'PoK Is Jallianwala Bagh, Asim Munir Is General Dwyer': Ex J&K Top Cop
content: Likening the situation in Pakistan-occupied Jammu and Kashmir (PoK) to the Jallianwala Bagh massacre, former Jammu and Kashmir Director General of Police SP Vaid accused Field Marshal General Asim Munir of acting like General Dyer.

title: 32,000 Displaced As Buildings Reduced To Rubble In Philippines Earthquake
content: Monday's earthquake was centered at sea at a depth of 33 kilometres, about 32 kilometres southwest of Maasim town in Sarangani province.

title: Opinion: Opinion | The Chinese Are Using LinkedIn 'Jobs' To Spy On Countries. Is India Prepared?
content: A recent report throws light on how Chinese operatives frequently disguise themselves as representatives of private consultancies, research institutions, or human resource firms, posting seemingly legitimate job opportunities for foreign policy, defe

title: Why Elon Musk Says This Once Top Job May Be Dead By This Year
content: Tesla and xAI boss Elon Musk is well-known for making forward-looking statements on AI and its impact on jobs and society.

title: BJP Leader Criticises Operation Bluestar In Big Shift In Punjab Strategy
content: For the first time, a senior BJP leader has publicly echoed sentiments traditionally associated with panthic politics.

title: India World's 5th Largest Military Spender With $92 Billion War Chest: Report
content: In its annual assessment report launched on Monday, the Stockholm International Peace Research Institute (SIPRI) also said that India has approximately 190 nuclear warheads as of January 2026, compared to Pakistan's estimated stockpile of 170.

title: Congress MLAs Stranded At Bhopal Airport Over Plane Mix-up
content: On Tuesday, the MLAs first gathered at Leader of Opposition Umang Singhar's residence and then left for the airport in their own vehicles.

title: After Mumbai Concert Death, Spotlight On Drugs, Safety Lapses
content: What may appear to be an immersive music experience or an all-night party can conceal serious risks, from substance abuse to life-threatening negligence.

title: Passports Held, Coerced Into Scams: How "Cyber Slavery" Gang Lured Indians
content: The CBI has arrested two key accused in connection with a human trafficking network that allegedly lured Indian nationals to cyber scam compounds in Southeast Asia

title: Opinion: Opinion | Why Trump's Attention Is Quickly Becoming Pak's Biggest Headache
content: Pakistan's real challenge may not be winning Trump's attention - that it has certainly been successful at. It may be surviving the consequences of that.

title: Sikkim Suicide Rate Falls 15.8% In 2 Years As Focus On Mental Health Expands
content: Welcoming the decline, Sikkim Health Minister GT Dhungel attributed the improvement to the combined efforts of the Health and Family Welfare Department and mental health professionals.

title: UK MPs Write To British Foreign Secretary Over PoK Crackdown, Blackout
content: Over 50 British Parliamentarians have urged Foreign Secretary Yvette Cooper to intervene as a communications blackout and security crackdown grip Pakistan-occupied Jammu and Kashmir.

title: Stock Market Highlights, Sensex Today: Sensex Jumps 400 Points As Traders Assess Fragile Iran Ceasefire
content: Stock Market, Sensex, Share Market, Nifty LIVE Updates: Oil prices eased marginally after Israel and Iran signalled a pause in hostilities.

title: Delhi Professor Offered Her Tenants Water, Then A Murderous Plot Played Out
content: The accused struck the teacher on her head with the pestle that he had in his backpack and then slit her wrists with a razor.
